channel.title: Extras :: SE How-To Build Lab
channel.description: Proxmox Install Guacamole Troubleshoot Ansible Nesting ESXi Windows Persistant Route Upgrade Existing OOB

item.title: Proxmox Install
item.description: “Official” Proxmox documentation PVE Docs PVE Wiki The following is “how I did it” and “lessons learned (the hard way).” Base Install Post Installation “Must Do’s”

item.title: Guacamole
item.description: Change/Add/Delete Guacamole Connections Open browswer and login to http://172.16.3.80:8080/guacamole User/Password: guacadmin / guacadmin Click on Settings Click on Connections Configure SSH Configure RDP (for Ubuntu) Configure RDP (for Windows) Add Guacamole URL’s to homepage Launch a connection from guacadmin / Home Copy the URL Add to homepage’s bookmark.yaml file /home/fortinet/c_data/homepage/config/bookmarks.yml From the homepage click on the link Source/Details Create the Guacamole database Create a user for Guacamole database Configure connection settings MySQL dump and restore

item.title: Troubleshoot Ansible
item.description: Below are common Ansible issues that require troubleshooting No Route to Host Network Connectivity Host reachable via ping (using IP address and hostname) DNS Resolution using hostname, resolves to correct IP address SSH Connection Via CLI: ssh @<target_ip_address> Via CLI: ssh @<target_hostname> If target is Proxmox server (i.e. deploying a new VM on Proxmox ) Check Firewall Rules (In this lab, Proxmox should have Firewall turned off) Check IP addresses match in the following files /etc/network/interfaces /etc/hosts If not, after making these the same, reboot Proxmox Check Self-Signed Certificate The self-signed certificate connection details (IP or hostname in its Subject Alternative Names list) must match IP address from previous step. If it doesn’t, execute the next two steps ​ from PVE Shell Force regeneration of self-cert pvecm updatecerts --force ​ from PVE Shell Restart the web interface services systemctl restart pvedaemon pveproxy

item.title: Nesting ESXi
item.description: Why would do this? FortiLink trunks do not work in Proxmox due to Proxmox developers have reserved the use of VLANs 0 and 4095 (need to find link defintively describin this). There are “how-to” posts that I could not make FortiLink work in Proxmox: (examples below) global channels Support Forum Verify Nested Virtualization is Enabled https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Nested_Virtualization https://devopstales.github.io/virtualization/install-vmware-in-proxmox/ At CLI: cat /sys/module/kvm_intel/parameters/nested If it returns “Y”, proceed to Creation Step If it returns “N”, follow steps in URL above to enable ESXi VM Creation In PVE GUI > Click “Create VM” General

item.title: Windows Persistant Route
item.description: Why would do this? VPN Split Tunnel (SASE) “blocking” access to SE Lab’s subnet. Adding a persistent network route in Windows 11 that survives reboots Syntax: route -p add [destination_network] mask [subnet_mask] [gateway_ip] Steps: Open Command Prompt as Admin: Right-click on CMD Example: route -p add 192.168.50.0 mask 255.255.255.0 192.168.1.1 Verify via CMD: route print

item.title: Upgrade Existing OOB
item.description: Why would do this? Rebuild OOB completely without losing Homepage, Guacamole, and Ansible configs Want OOB built off a newer Ubuntu Template VM Want latest Automation Ansible Build Scripts Backup Config Guacamole ​ from Terminal in /home/Fortinet/c_data/guacamole docker compose ps docker compose exec guacamole-sql /bin/sh sh-5.1# mysqldump -u root -p --all-databases > backup.sql Enter password: password sh-5.1# exit docker compose cp guacamole-sql:/backup.sql ./backup.sql
